steps.title: Identify the underlined claim's causal logic
steps.content: The underlined claim attributes backlash to the art's own unconventional nature—public art that isn't sufficiently bland provokes backlash by failing to appease everyone.

steps.title: Apply Text 2's evidence to the correct answer
steps.content:
The matching choice asserts that reactions to controversial public art are not exclusively caused by inherent attributes of the works.

Text 2 supports this by emphasizing context: norm-defying public art may be judged harshly when it is poorly integrated into its surroundings, while museum settings can prepare viewers to receive challenging art more favorably.

That means the public setting and presentation affect reception along with the work's provocativeness.

The other choices either deny unconventionality's role (A), contradict Text 2 (B), or target a different claim (C).

D is correct.
